problem.statement: The sovereign Platform section previously mixed routine DSH operations with platform control, exposed static states, and lacked a persistent maker-checker workflow for variables and feature flags.
problem.currentWorkaround: No workaround is acceptable; sovereign changes must use platform-control and fail closed when persistence, authorization, audit, or rollback is unavailable.

actors.role: Highest administrative authority that reads the complete control-plane posture and governs role assignment through Administration.
permittedActions: Read health and audit when separately authorized
forbiddenActions: Perform routine catalog marketing support zone SLA HR partner or order operations

actors.role: Maker that drafts validates and submits sovereign changes.
forbiddenActions: Apply or roll back without dedicated permission

actors.role: Independent checker that approves or rejects submitted changes.
permittedActions: Approve a change proposed by another actor
Reject a submitted change with a reason

actors.role: Dedicated executor that atomically applies or rolls back independently approved change sets.
forbiddenActions: Overwrite a newer revision during apply or rollback

actors.role: Independent reader of immutable workflow and runtime evidence.
forbiddenActions: Propose approve apply reject or roll back changes

actions: approve or reject when independently authorized
apply or rollback when independently authorized
read back workflow and effective state after mutation

surfaces.exclusionReason: Customers consume effective outcomes only and must never access sovereign controls.
Partners consume effective outcomes only and must never access sovereign controls.
Captains consume effective outcomes only and must never access sovereign controls.
Field staff may not view or mutate sovereign platform controls.

outcome.statement: Authorized executive actors use one persistent platform-control plane with truthful reads, separated duties, audited changes, effective readback, and conflict-safe rollback while routine domains remain outside Platform.
outcome.baselineState: Platform was a partially bound read-only view with local/static models and no persistent governed mutation workflow.
outcome.targetState: Every active read comes from platform-control; every supported variable or flag mutation passes proposal validation independent approval atomic apply audit readback and rollback.
outcome.observationWindow: Every platform-control request, workflow transition, runtime smoke, and release verification.

criteria: Platform navigation and every backend route require a control-panel platform permission.
Variables feature flags services health providers audit and change sets use live APIs and shared controllers.
Operator approver and applier permissions are separated and an actor cannot approve its own proposal.
A valid change passes draft validated submitted approved applied and rolled_back states with persisted readback.
Apply and rollback reject stale revisions and execute in serializable PostgreSQL transactions.
Every workflow transition creates a persisted audit event with actor roles status and correlation id.
Routine zone SLA onboarding-fee notification catalog marketing support HR and partner controls are absent from Platform.
Unsupported progressive rollout actions remain explicitly CONTRACT_REQUIRED with no simulation.
Partial read failures remain visible without discarding successful reads.
The Docker runtime starts identity PostgreSQL and platform-control and the smoke journey passes with separated actors.
failureStates: A static registry value is presented as live platform truth.
A routine operational screen is rendered in Platform.
A user without platform:read sees platform data or navigation.
A permission issued for another surface authorizes platform-control.
The proposer can approve its own change.
A mutation writes outside platform-control or without a transaction.
Apply or rollback overwrites a newer revision.
A successful mutation lacks audit or affected-state readback.
A rollout action appears active while the rollout backend is not implemented.
Runtime health or readiness claims success while the platform database is unavailable.

business: Platform is a sovereign executive control plane and never a daily operations workspace.
Administration owns role assignment while platform-control enforces permissions and separation of duties.
WLT remains the only owner of financial truth.
Provider secrets remain backend-only and masked.
Every displayed state comes from a live owner API or is labelled unavailable restricted or contract-required.
Every supported mutation has proposal validation independent approval apply audit readback and rollback.
negative: No customer partner captain or field surface exposes Platform controls.
No local React state fixture seed fallback or registry constant is presented as live platform truth.
No actor approves its own proposal.
No platform mutation bypasses platform-control PostgreSQL transactions.
No rollback executes without a captured before-state and matching applied revision.
No zone SLA catalog marketing support HR partner order or financial routine operation is owned by Platform.
No health or audit data is fetched without its dedicated permission.
No progressive rollout is claimed operational until targeting health gates pause abort and rollback are implemented and proven.

execution.appetite: Full-stack governed implementation of the sovereign control plane without activating unimplemented progressive delivery or production release claims.
fixedConstraints: Use GitHub Remote branch bassam as implementation truth.
Do not create a second platform truth source.
Require separated maker checker and applier authority.
Require runtime and visual evidence before closure.
variableScope: Progressive rollout implementation after its own governed model
forbiddenScope: Production release or final closure without independent approvals
Local demo mutation or fake runtime data
execution.forecastRange: Multi-commit full-stack closure journey on branch bassam.
unknowns: Live visual evidence from the operator environment
Progressive rollout provider selection and operational acceptance
